evals.prompt: We're building an ambient AI scribe for ambulatory primary care. It records the visit, generates a SOAP note draft, and the physician edits and signs. Walk us through what we need to design.
evals.expected_output: Should read healthcare-context.md or ask about EHR target. Should structure the response around microphone-to-signed-note workflow: launch (often via SMART), consent capture (HIPAA NPP plus explicit patient consent, with attention to state two-party consent laws), audio streaming under a BAA, draft generation (call out APSO vs SOAP and that the A&P is the most error-prone section), clinician review in scribe UI or EHR, FHIR DocumentReference write-back (or vendor-specific note API), and audio retention per the BAA. Should warn about negation handling in NLP, copy-forward, and measuring clinician edit rate rather than only WER. Should reference hipaa-compliance, fhir-integration, ehr-integration, clinical-ai-ml.
assertions: Walks through microphone-to-signed-note workflow
Addresses consent including state two-party laws
Mentions BAA-covered audio infrastructure
Notes APSO vs SOAP and A&P risk
Recommends FHIR DocumentReference write-back
Warns about copy-forward / note bloat
Mentions measuring clinician edit rate not just WER
References hipaa-compliance and clinical-ai-ml

evals.prompt: Give me a SOAP and an APSO template for an inpatient progress note that we can use as a starting point.
evals.expected_output: Should provide both SOAP and APSO progress note templates with the canonical section ordering and brief guidance per section. SOAP: Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan. APSO: Assessment, Plan, Subjective, Objective. Should explain APSO is increasingly preferred so the next reader sees the conclusion first. Should reference references/note-templates.md if available. Should remind that LOINC document type codes must be verified against the current LOINC release and the customer's site mapping.
assertions: Provides full SOAP template with all four sections
Provides full APSO template with all four sections
Explains APSO rationale (reader sees A&P first)
References note-templates.md or equivalent reference
Notes LOINC document type codes must be verified

evals.prompt: we want to run nlp on free-text notes to extract problems and meds. what do we use
evals.expected_output: Should trigger on casual phrasing. Should compare open-source options (cTAKES, MedSpaCy, MetaMap) and managed services (Amazon Comprehend Medical, AWS HealthLake, Google Healthcare NLP API, Azure AI Language for Health), noting their typical strengths. Should describe a canonical pipeline: de-identification, sentence/section detection, NER, assertion (negation, hypothetical, family member, historical), concept normalization to UMLS / SNOMED CT / RxNorm / LOINC, relationship extraction, FHIR output. Should stress that negation is non-negotiable. Should recommend evaluation against a clinician-adjudicated reference set with F1 stratified by section. Should reference phi-handling for de-identification.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing
Compares open-source and managed NLP options
Describes a canonical pipeline including de-identification
Stresses negation / assertion handling
Recommends F1 evaluation against clinician-adjudicated set
References phi-handling skill

evals.prompt: Design end-to-end documentation for a multi-day inpatient stay: admission H&P, daily progress notes, consult notes, discharge summary. What templates, what structured data, how does it all flow?
evals.expected_output: Should describe each note type (H&P at admission, progress note daily in APSO, consult note when consulted, discharge summary with course / final diagnoses / discharge meds / follow-up). Should identify which structured FHIR data is captured at each step (Condition for problems, Observation for vitals and labs, AllergyIntolerance, Procedure, Immunization, MedicationRequest, FamilyMemberHistory, social-history Observations). Should warn about copy-forward across daily notes and the documentation-integrity risks. Should describe write-back via DocumentReference, with docStatus progressing from preliminary to final on sign-off. Should mention attribution and co-signature for residents and students. Should reference fhir-integration, ehr-integration, terminology-services, medication-reconciliation for discharge med list.
assertions: Walks through H&P, progress, consult, and discharge notes
Names structured FHIR resources captured at each step
Warns about copy-forward across daily notes
Describes docStatus preliminary -> final on sign-off
Addresses attribution and resident co-signature
References medication-reconciliation for discharge meds
References fhir-integration and terminology-services

evals.prompt: What are the HIPAA breach-notification rules if a clinical note containing PHI is accidentally emailed to the wrong patient?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a HIPAA breach-notification question, not a clinical documentation workflow design question. Should defer to or cross-reference the hipaa-compliance skill, which covers the HIPAA Breach Notification Rule, the four-factor risk assessment, and notification timelines. Should not attempt the full breach analysis inside clinical-documentation. May briefly note that documentation tools should support audit and access logs (audit-logging) to support breach investigations.
assertions: Recognizes this is a HIPAA breach-notification question
Defers to or references hipaa-compliance skill
References audit-logging skill
Does not attempt full breach analysis inside this skill

evals.prompt: We need to define SmartPhrases / dot phrases for our ED residency that auto-populate the HPI and ROS. Should we standardize them across all residents?
evals.expected_output: Should explain SmartPhrase / dot phrase mechanics (Epic SmartPhrase, Cerner AutoText, Athenahealth macros) and the difference between personal and shared/institutional libraries. Should warn that copy-forward and over-templated normal exams are a top documentation-integrity problem and a payer-dispute source. Should recommend a curated institutional library for high-volume use cases combined with the ability for clinicians to override. Should suggest tracking usage and the percentage of note content that is truly new. Should not invent specific vendor SmartPhrases. Should reference ehr-integration for site-specific templating tools.
assertions: Explains SmartPhrase / dot phrase mechanics
Distinguishes personal vs institutional libraries
Warns about copy-forward and templated-exam pitfalls
Recommends measuring new-content percentage
Does not invent specific vendor SmartPhrases
References ehr-integration skill
